subject: Statistics
title: Hierarchical Models
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Hierarchical Models.

hook.text: Ten schools test a new curriculum. Results vary wildly. Should you analyze them separately or together?

buildup.text: Hierarchical models do both. Each school has its own effect, but all effects are drawn from a shared distribution.

discovery.text: Small schools borrow strength from the group. Their extreme results get pulled toward the overall average.

twist.text: This 'shrinkage' prevents overreacting to noisy small samples while respecting genuine differences.

climax.text: Hierarchical models are uniquely Bayesian. They handle grouped data more honestly than any frequentist method.

punchline.text: Share information across groups. That's the hierarchy's power.

quiz.question: What do hierarchical Bayesian models achieve?
options: They balance individual group estimates with the overall pattern, reducing noise in small groups
They eliminate all variance between groups
They ignore differences between groups entirely
